summary: Matched the first two Samus source files by moving a large block of Samus assembly into C, renaming Samus symbols to ftSamus_8012xxxx form, splitting the former ftsamus.c into ftsamus1.c plus new ftsamus2.c, and updating build objects, headers, action-state table references, and external asm callers. The PR removed about 713 lines from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftsamus.s and added C implementations for Samus accessory animation setup, grapple/transition-related helpers, action callbacks, physics callbacks, and state transitions. Review evidence is minimal beyond an approval.

key_files.role: Removed matched Samus assembly and changed action-state table callback pointers from lbl_80128xxx labels to ftSamus_80128xxx C symbols.
Renamed from ftsamus.c and extended with ftSamus_80128770 and ftSamus_801287C4 for accessory/JObj animation setup.

New matched C file containing ftSamus_80128944, ftSamus_80128A1C, ftSamus_80128AC8, ftSamus_80128B1C, action callbacks ftSamus_80128C04/80128CA0/80128D3C/80128E68/80128E88/80128EF8/80128F60/80128FD4, and state-transition helpers ftSamus_80129048/801290A4.
Added item include, a provisional UNK_SAMUS_S1 struct describing joint/anim/matanim fields, and declarations for ftSamus_80129048 and ftSamus_801290A4.
Split Fighter offset 0x5F4 from one s32 into four s8 fields x5F4, x5F5, x5F6, x5F7 to support byte access used by Samus code.
Updated function declarations/prototype comments, including func_80008248 and ftSamus_80128AC8/ftSamus_80128B1C naming.
Replaced ftsamus.c.o with ftsamus1.c.o and ftsamus2.c.o in TEXT_O_FILES.
Updated external branch target from func_801287C4 to ftSamus_801287C4.
Updated item-side calls from func_80128A1C/func_80128944 to ftSamus_80128A1C/ftSamus_80128944.
Changed one assert string from literal "ftyoshi.c" to __FILE__, likely for matching or consistency.

what_changed: Converted the first Samus assembly region, starting around lbl_80128770 through func_801290A4, into C implementations split across ftsamus1.c and ftsamus2.c.

Kept later Samus assembly in ftsamus.s, but removed the matched text and associated local sdata/sdata2 constants such as lbl_804D3E30, lbl_804D3E38, lbl_804D9994, lbl_804D9998, lbl_804D999C, lbl_804D99A0, and lbl_804D99A4 from the asm file.
Renamed action-state table callbacks in as_table_samus from lbl_80128C04/lbl_80128D3C/lbl_80128E88/lbl_80128F60 and lbl_80128CA0/lbl_80128E68/lbl_80128EF8/lbl_80128FD4 to ftSamus_ prefixed C symbols.
Added ftSamus_801287C4, which sets up Samus accessory JObj scale and animation using HSD_JObjAddAnimAll, HSD_ForeachAnim, HSD_JObjReqAnimAll, HSD_JObjAnimAll, and func_8000C2F8.
Added ftSamus_80128A1C hurtbox iteration logic using fighter->x119E_hurtboxNum, fighter->x11A0_fighterHurtbox, func_8007F804, and func_80008248.
Added ftSamus_80128AC8 angle calculation/clamping logic based on fighter x-position, two float arguments, and ftSamusAttributes x4.
Added ftSamus_80128B1C and inline helper logic that sets self velocity using cosf/sinf, clears ftcmd/state variables, optionally calls func_8007D5D4 on ground, and changes action state to 0x156.
Added ground/air callbacks for action states 0x127 and 0x128, including ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining checks and transitions through func_8008A2BC or func_800CC730.

Added environment/transition callbacks that call common fighter movement and collision helpers such as func_80096540, func_800D695C, func_800D6824, func_800D68C0, func_800D8990, func_8008BFC4, func_8008C830, func_8008CB44, func_8008B658, func_8008B980, func_8008BB44, func_8008A9F8, and func_80099794.
Adjusted Fighter layout at offsets 0x5F4-0x5F7 to byte fields, enabling C to match lbz access to fighter->x5F5.
smart_moves: Preserved external asm compatibility by updating branch labels in unrelated asm files to the new ftSamus_ symbol names rather than leaving stale func_8012xxxx references.
Split the Samus work into ftsamus1.c and ftsamus2.c and updated obj_files.mk accordingly, making the matched region map cleanly to separate source objects.

Used existing typed fighter fields where available, such as x20A0_accessory, x5E8_fighterBones, x119E_hurtboxNum, x11A0_fighterHurtbox, x2200_ftcmd_var0, x2204_ftcmd_var1, and x2340_stateVar1, instead of only raw offsets.
Introduced a small provisional struct, UNK_SAMUS_S1, for the Samus item/accessory animation data instead of opaque pointer arithmetic at every access.
Matched byte-level assembly behavior by changing Fighter offset 0x5F4 from a single s32 to four s8 fields, allowing direct use of fighter->x5F5.
Kept uncertain knowledge explicit: the new struct is documented only as "maybe Samus grapple?", which avoids overclaiming the semantics.
decomp_lessons: When C code needs to reproduce lbz/stb access to a subfield inside a formerly word-sized unknown, split the struct field into byte-sized members rather than forcing casts.
For fighter action-state tables, matched C callback names must be propagated back into the remaining assembly table entries, or links/calls will still target removed labels.
When moving only part of a character file out of asm, also remove or relocate local constants in .sdata/.sdata2 that belonged to the matched C functions.

Accessory/JObj setup code can often be made readable by naming the data bundle struct around observed HSD_Joint, HSD_AnimJoint, and HSD_MatAnimJoint fields even before its final semantic name is known.
C prototypes in include/functions.h can be incrementally improved during matching; this PR promoted func_80008248 and Samus helper signatures enough for the matched code to compile.
Use action-state IDs, frame-preservation arguments, and exact helper-call order from assembly when implementing state-transition helpers such as ftSamus_80129048 and ftSamus_801290A4.
naming_conventions: Samus-specific unknown functions were renamed from generic func_8012xxxx/lbl_8012xxxx names to ftSamus_8012xxxx.
The new source files use lowercase filenames ftsamus1.c and ftsamus2.c under the existing ftSamus directory.
Unconfirmed structs can use an UNK_ prefix with a short subsystem tag, as in UNK_SAMUS_S1, plus a cautionary comment.
Fighter struct fields retained offset-based names, e.g. x5F5, x2200_ftcmd_var0, x2204_ftcmd_var1, and x2340_stateVar1, where semantic names were not yet established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used a callback helper ftSamus_80128770 passed to HSD_ForeachAnim to match the assembly sequence that conditionally calls HSD_AObjSetRate unless HSD_AObjGetFlags has bit 0x20000000 set.
Matched clamping assembly in ftSamus_80128AC8 with explicit if checks for value >= 1.0f and value <= -1.0f before returning (-attr->x4 * value) + 1.5707963705062866f.
Introduced ftSamus_80128B1C_inner as an inline helper to structure the velocity/cosf/sinf and aerial drift calculations while preserving generated code shape.
Preserved action-state transition parameters, including states 0x156 and 0x155, flag 0x10 in transition helpers, current animation frame, frame speed multiplier, and 0.0f blend/unknown float.
Matched assembly switch behavior in ftSamus_80128944 by switching on fighter->x2071_b0_3 and handling cases 0, 2, 3, and 4 while omitting case 1 and values outside the assembly range.
The Yoshi assert change from a string literal to __FILE__ suggests matching sometimes depends on compiler-emitted file string handling, even for unrelated nearby files.

review_feedback.note: No inline review comments or written reviewer rationale were present in the PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftSamus_801287C4, ftSamus_80128A1C, or ftSamus_80128B1C to semantic names.
Search for follow-up changes to UNK_SAMUS_S1 or confirmation that it represents Samus grapple/accessory animation data.
Look for later Fighter struct refinements around offsets 0x5F4-0x5F7 and whether x5F5 gains a semantic name.
Find PRs that continue decompiling the remaining asm/melee/ft/chara/ftsamus.s functions after func_80129100.
Search for other PRs using __FILE__ in asserts for matching, especially around ftyoshi.c.
